description_long: This research institute is a large, rectangular building complex with a modern, utilitarian design. The structure features a combination of flat and slightly sloped roofs, with several skylights or roof panels visible along the central section. The exterior is primarily finished in light gray and blue tones, with large window panels and a few distinct entry points, including a prominent main entrance with a canopy. The building is composed of multiple connected sections, including a long wing and a more compact, darker-roofed area, likely for specialized research or administrative functions. The overall layout suggests a focus on functionality, with clear separations between different operational zones and accessible pathways. The materials and design indicate durability and practicality, suitable for an urban academic or scientific environment.
description: A large, modern research institute with flat roofs, skylights, and a mix of gray and blue exterior panels. The building features multiple connected sections and a prominent main entrance with a canopy.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the length of the building and the alignment of the main sections, with the roof details and skylights visible.
description_view_1: A broad side view highlighting the main facade, large window panels, and the prominent entrance canopy on the right.
description_view_2: A rear or opposite side view showing the compact, darker-roofed section and the overall rectangular footprint of the building.
description_view_3: Another broad side view, similar to view 1 but from the opposite direction, showing the long wing and additional entry points.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway features a wide, flat canopy extending over a set of double doors. The entrance is flush with the facade, accessible at ground level, and framed by large glass panels. There are no visible steps, suggesting wheelchair accessibility, and the design is minimalistic with no decorative elements.
